title: Methods and systems for phasing sequencing strands and longârange sequencing
abstract: Described herein are methods synchronizing sequencing primers within a sequencing cluster and methods of generating long-range sequencing reads. The methods can include hybridizing primers to polynucleotide copies within a sequencing cluster; extending the primers through a first region of the polynucleotide copies using labeled nucleotides according to a sequencing flow order; extending the primers through a second region of the polynucleotide copies using one or more re-phasing flow steps that each include at least two different types of nucleotide bases; and extending the primers through a third region of the polynucleotide copies using labeled nucleotides according to the sequencing cycle. The rephasing flow steps may be initiated after a predetermined number of sequencing flow steps, after a measured sequencing signal strength falls below a predetermined sequencing signal strength threshold, or a measured sequencing signal-to-noise ratio falls below a sequencing signal-to-noise ratio threshold.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method of synchronizing sequencing in a colony, comprising: (a) providing a sequencing colony comprising a plurality of copies of nucleic acid molecules each having sequence identity to a polynucleotide derived from a sample, wherein each nucleic acid molecule of the plurality of copies comprises a first region, a second region, and a third region; (b) hybridizing a plurality of primers to the plurality of copies; (c) extending the plurality of primers through the first region by, in each flow step of a plurality of first flow cycles having a first predetermined number of flow steps, providing a first plurality of nucleotides of a single base type, wherein at least a portion of the first plurality of nucleotides is labeled; (d) extending, after the first predetermined number of flow steps, the plurality of primers through the second region by, in each flow step of a plurality of second flow cycles, providing a second plurality of nucleotides comprising at least two base types; and (e) extending the plurality of primers through the third region by, in each flow step of a plurality of third flow cycles, providing a third plurality of nucleotides of a single base type, wherein at least a portion of the third plurality of nucleotides is labeled. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the first predetermined number of flow steps is between about 40 and about 500. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the first predetermined number of flow steps is associated with a predetermined sequencing signal threshold. 4. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the first predetermined number of flow steps is associated with an expected proportion of unsynchronized primers. 5. The method of claim 4, wherein the expected proportion of unsynchronized primers is between about 0.1 and about 0.5. 6. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the first predetermined number of sequencing flow steps is associated with an expected length of the first region. 48 7. The method of any one of claims 1-6, wherein the plurality of third flow cycles has a second predetermined number of flow steps, the method further comprising: (f) extending, after the second predetermined number of flow steps, the plurality of primers through a fourth region of the plurality of copies by, in each flow step of a plurality of fourth flow cycles, providing a fourth plurality of nucleotides comprising at least two base types; and (g) extending the plurality of primers through a fifth region of the plurality of copies by, in each flow step of a plurality of fifth flow cycles, providing a fifth plurality of nucleotides of a single base type, wherein at least a portion of the fifth plurality of nucleotides is labeled. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the first predetermined number of flow steps and the second predetermined number of flow steps are the same. 9. The method of claim 7, wherein the second predetermined number of flow steps is less than the first predetermined number of flow steps. 10. The method of any one of claims 7-9, comprising sequencing the fifth region by detecting the presence or absence of an incorporated labeled nucleotide while extending the primers through the fifth region. 11. A method of synchronizing sequencing, comprising: (a) providing a sequencing colony comprising a plurality of copies of nucleic acid molecules each having sequence identity to a polynucleotide derived from a sample, wherein each nucleic acid molecule of the plurality of copies comprises a first region, a second region, and a third region; (b) hybridizing a plurality of primers to the plurality of copies; (c) extending the plurality of primers through the first region by, in each flow step of a plurality of first flow cycles, (i) providing a first plurality of nucleotides of a single base type, wherein at least a portion of the first plurality of nucleotides are labeled, and (ii) detecting a signal indicative of incorporation, or lack thereof, of a labeled nucleotide of the first plurality of nucleotides in the plurality of primers, until the signal falls below a first predetermined sequencing signal threshold; 49 

 (d) extending, after the signal falls below the first predetermined sequencing signal threshold, the plurality of primers through the second region by, in each flow step of a plurality of second flow cycles, providing a second plurality of nucleotides comprising at least two base types; and (e) extending the plurality of primers through the third region by, in each flow step of a plurality of third flow cycles, providing a third plurality of nucleotides of a single base type, wherein at least a portion of the third plurality of nucleotides is labeled. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the signal is a sequencing signal intensity or a sequencing signal-to-noise ratio. 13. The method of claim 12, further comprising detecting one or more signals or sequencing signal-to-noise ratio as the primers are extended through the first region in (c). 14. The method of any one of claims 11-13, wherein the plurality of primers is extended through the third region until a second signal detected falls below a second predetermined sequencing signal threshold, the method further comprising: (f) extending, after the second signal falls below the second predetermined sequencing signal threshold, the plurality of primers through a fourth region of the plurality of copies by, in each flow step of a plurality of fourth flow cycles, providing a fourth plurality of nucleotides comprising at least two base types; and (g) extending the plurality of primers through a fifth region of the plurality of copies by, in each flow step of a plurality of fifth flow cycles, providing a fifth plurality of nucleotides of a single base type, wherein at least a portion of the fifth plurality of nucleotides is labeled. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein the first predetermined sequencing signal threshold and the second predetermined sequencing signal threshold are the same. 16. The method of claim 14 or 15, further comprising sequencing the fifth region by detecting the presence or absence of an incorporated labeled nucleotide while extending the primers through the fifth region. 50 17. The method of any one of claims 1-16, wherein a mixture of three different base types is used in at least one flow step of the plurality of second flow cycles. 18. The method of any one of claims 1-17, wherein one or more flow steps of the plurality of second flow cycles comprise 2 to 12 re-phasing flow steps. 19. The method of any one of claims 1-18, wherein one or more flow steps of the plurality of second flow cycles comprise 3 or more re-phasing flow steps. 20. The method of any one of claims 1-19, wherein one or more flow steps of the plurality of second flow cycles comprise 3 re-phasing flow steps. 21. The method of any one of claims 1-20, wherein one or more flow steps of the plurality of second flow cycles comprise one or more of the following in any order: (i) a flow step comprising a mixture comprising A, C, and G nucleotides and omitting T nucleotides; (ii) a flow step comprising a mixture comprising T, C, and G nucleotides and omitting A nucleotides; (ii) a flow step comprising a mixture comprising T, A, and G nucleotides and omitting C nucleotides; and (iv) a flow step comprising a mixture comprising T, A, and C nucleotides and omitting G nucleotides. 22. The method of any one of claims 1-21, further comprising sequencing the first region by detecting the presence or absence of an incorporated labeled nucleotide while extending the primers through the first region. 23. The method of any one of claims 1-21, comprising sequencing the third region by detecting the presence or absence of an incorporated labeled nucleotide while extending the primers through the third region. 24. The method of any one of claims 1-23, wherein at least a portion of nucleotides provided in the plurality of second flow cycles are unlabeled. 51 25. The method of any one of claims 1-24, wherein a first flow order of the plurality of first flow cycles and a third order of the plurality of third flow cycles are the same. 26. The method of any one of claims 1-24, wherein a first flow order of the plurality of first flow cycles and a third order of the plurality of third flow cycles are different. 27. The method of any one of claims 1-26, wherein the plurality of primers is extended through the first region by repeating a first flow order a plurality of times in the plurality of first flow cycles. 28. The method of claim 27, wherein the first flow order is repeated 2 times to about 50 times. 29. The method of any one of claims 1-28, wherein the plurality of primers is extended through the third region by repeating a third flow order a plurality of times in the plurality of third flow cycles. 30. The method of claim 29, wherein the third flow order is repeated 2 times to about 50 times. 31. The method of any one of claims 1-30, wherein the plurality of primers is extended through three or more separate regions of the plurality of copies using a plurality of rephasing flow steps, and wherein sequencing data is generated for four or more separate regions of the polynucleotide copies. 32. The method of any one of claims 1-31, wherein a distance between a start of the first region and an end of a final region of the plurality of copies for which sequencing data is generated is at least 300 bases in length. 33. A method of synchronizing sequencing primers within a sequencing cluster, comprising: hybridizing primers to polynucleotide copies within the sequencing cluster; extending the primers through a first region of the polynucleotide copies using nucleotides provided according to a first region flow order for a first predetermined number of sequencing flow steps, wherein, during each sequencing flow step in the first region flow 

order, a single type of nucleotide base is used and at least a portion of the nucleotides are labeled; extending, after the first predetermined number of sequencing flow steps, the primers through a second region of the polynucleotide copies using one or more re-phasing flow steps, wherein, during each of the one or more re-phasing flow steps, at least two different types of nucleotide bases are simultaneously used; and extending the primers through a third region of the polynucleotide copies using nucleotides provided according to a third region flow order comprising a plurality of sequencing flow steps, wherein, during each sequencing flow step in the third region flow order, a single type of nucleotide base is used and at least a portion of the nucleotides are labeled. 34. The method of claim 33, wherein the first predetermined number of sequencing flows is between about 40 and about 500. 35. The method of claim 33 or 34, wherein the first predetermined number of sequencing flow steps is associated with a predetermined sequencing signal threshold. 36. The method of claim 33 or 34, wherein the first predetermined number of sequencing flow steps is associated with an expected proportion of unsynchronized primers. 37. The method of claim 36, wherein the expected proportion of unsynchronized primers is between about 0.1 and about 0.5. 38. The method of claim 33 or 34, wherein the first predetermined number of sequencing flow steps is associated with an expected length of the first region. 39. The method of any one of claims 33-38, further comprising: extending, after the primers are extended through the third region for a second predetermined number of sequencing flow steps, the primers through a fourth region of the polynucleotide copies using one or more re-phasing flow steps, wherein, during each of the one or more re-phasing flow steps, at least two different types of nucleotide bases are simultaneously used; and 

 extending the primers through a fifth region of the polynucleotide copies using nucleotides provided according to a fifth region flow order comprising a plurality of sequencing flow steps, wherein, during each sequencing flow step in the fifth region flow order, a single type of nucleotide base is used and at least a portion of the nucleotides are labeled. 40. The method of claim 39, wherein the first predetermined number of sequencing flow steps and the second predetermined number of sequencing flow steps are the same. 41. The method of claim 39, wherein the second predetermined number of sequencing flow steps is less than the first predetermined number of sequencing flow steps. 42. The method of any one of claims 39-41, comprising generating sequencing data associated with a sequence of the fifth region by detecting the presence or absence of an incorporated labeled nucleotide while extending the primers through the fifth region. 43. A method of synchronizing sequencing primers within a sequencing cluster, comprising: hybridizing primers to polynucleotide copies within the sequencing cluster; extending the primers through a first region of the polynucleotide copies using nucleotides provided according to a first region flow order comprising a plurality of sequencing flow steps until a sequencing signal falls below a first predetermined sequencing signal threshold, wherein, during each sequencing flow step in the first region flow order, a single type of nucleotide base is used and at least a portion of the nucleotides are labeled; extending, after the sequencing signal falls below the first predetermined sequencing signal threshold, the primers through a second region of the polynucleotide copies using one or more re-phasing flow steps, wherein, during each of the one or more re-phasing flow steps, at least two different types of nucleotide bases are simultaneously used; and extending the primers through a third region of the polynucleotide copies using nucleotides provided according to a third region flow order comprising a plurality of sequencing flow steps, wherein, during each sequencing flow step in the third region flow order, a single type of nucleotide base is used and at least a portion of the nucleotides are labeled. 54 44. The method of claim 43, wherein the sequencing signal is a sequencing signal intensity or a sequencing signal-to-noise ratio. 45. The method of claim 44, wherein the sequencing signal or sequencing signal-to-noise ratio is measured as the primers are extended through the first region. 46. The method of any one of claims 43-45, further comprising: extending, after the primers are extended through the third region until a second sequencing signal falls below a second predetermined sequencing signal threshold, the primers through a fourth region of the polynucleotide copies using one or more re-phasing flow steps, wherein, during each of the one or more re-phasing flow steps, at least two different types of nucleotide bases are simultaneously used; and extending the primers through a fifth region of the polynucleotide copies using nucleotides provided according to a fifth region flow order comprising a plurality of sequencing flow steps, wherein, during each sequencing flow step in the fifth region flow order, a single type of nucleotide base is used and at least a portion of the nucleotides are labeled. 47. The method of claim 46, wherein the first predetermined sequencing signal threshold and the second predetermined sequencing signal threshold are the same. 48. The method of claim 46 or 47, comprising generating sequencing data associated with a sequence of the fifth region by detecting the presence or absence of an incorporated labeled nucleotide while extending the primers through the fifth region. 49. The method of any one of claims 33-48, wherein a mixture of three different types of nucleotide bases are used in at least one of the one or more re-phasing flow steps. 50. The method of any one of claims 33-49, wherein the one or more re-phasing flow steps comprises 2 to 12 re-phasing flow steps. 51. The method of any one of claims 33-50, wherein the one or more re-phasing flow steps comprises 3 or more re-phasing flow steps. 55 52. The method of any one of claims 33-51, wherein the one or more re-phasing flow steps comprises 3 re-phasing flow steps. 53. The method of any one of claims 33-52, wherein the one or more re-phasing flow steps comprises one or more of the following in any order: (i) a flow step comprising a mixture comprising A, C, and G nucleotides and omitting T nucleotides; (ii) a flow step comprising a mixture comprising T, C, and G nucleotides and omitting A nucleotides; (ii) a flow step comprising a mixture comprising T, A, and G nucleotides and omitting C nucleotides; and (iv) a flow step comprising a mixture comprising T, A, and C nucleotides and omitting G nucleotides. 54. The method of any one of claims 33-53, comprising generating sequencing data associated with a sequence of the first region by detecting the presence or absence of an incorporated labeled nucleotide while extending the primers through the first region. 55. The method of any one of claims 33-53, comprising generating sequencing data associated with a sequence of the third region by detecting the presence or absence of an incorporated labeled nucleotide while extending the primers through the third region. 56. The method of any one of claims 33-55, wherein at least a portion of nucleotides in the one or more re-phasing flow steps are unlabeled. 57. The method of any one of claims 33-56, wherein nucleotides in the one or more rephasing flow steps are unlabeled. 58. The method of any one of claims 33-57, wherein the first region flow order and the third region flow order are the same. 59. The method of any one of claims 33-57, wherein the first region flow order and the third region flow order are different. 56 60. The method of any one of claims 33-59, wherein the primers are extended through the first region of the polynucleotide copies by repeating the first region flow order a plurality of times. 61. The method of claim 60, wherein the first region flow order is repeated 2 times to about 50 times. 62. The method of any one of claims 33-61, wherein the primers are extended through the third region of the polynucleotide copies by repeating the third region flow order a plurality of times. 63. The method of claim 62, wherein the third region flow order is repeated 2 times to about 50 times. 64. The method of any one of claims 33-63, wherein the primers are extended through three or more separate regions of the polynucleotide copies using re-phasing flow steps, and wherein sequencing data is generated for four or more separate regions of the polynucleotide copies. 65. The method of any one of claims 33-64, wherein the distance between the start of the first region of the polynucleotide copies and the end of a final region of the polynucleotide copies for which sequencing data is generated is at least 300 bases in length. 66. A method of generating a long-range sequencing read, comprising: hybridizing primers to polynucleotide copies within a sequencing cluster; and altematingly generating sequencing data and re-phrasing the primers across a plurality of sequencing regions and a plurality of re-phrasing regions, wherein: generating sequencing data for a sequencing region comprises extending the primers through the sequencing region using nucleotides provided according to a sequencing flow order comprising a plurality of sequencing flow steps, wherein, during each sequencing flow step in the sequencing flow order, a single type of nucleotide base is used and at least a portion of the nucleotides are labeled, and detecting the presence or absence of an incorporated labeled nucleotide while extending the primers through the sequencing region; and 57 

re-phrasing the primers comprises extending the primers through a re-phasing region using one or more re-phasing flow steps, wherein, during each of the one or more re-phasing flow steps, at least two different types of nucleotide bases are simultaneously used; wherein the distance between the start of a first sequencing region and the end of a final sequencing region is at least 300 bases in length. 67. The method of claim 66, wherein the one or more re-phasing flow steps comprises between 2 and 12 re-phasing flow steps. 68. The method of claim 66 or 67, wherein the one or more re-phasing flow steps comprises 3 or more re-phasing flow steps. 69. The method of any one of claims 66-68, wherein the one or more re-phasing flow steps comprises 3 re-phasing flow steps. 70. The method of any one of claims 66-69, wherein the one or more re-phasing flow steps comprises one or more of the following in any order: (i) a flow step comprising a mixture comprising A, C, and G nucleotides and omitting T nucleotides; (ii) a flow step comprising a mixture comprising T, C, and G nucleotides and omitting A nucleotides; (ii) a flow step comprising a mixture comprising T, A, and G nucleotides and omitting C nucleotides; and (iv) a flow step comprising a mixture comprising T, A, and C nucleotides and omitting G nucleotides. 71. The method of any one of claims 66-70, each sequencing region is between about 50 bases in length and about 500 bases in length. 72. The method of any one of claims 66-71, wherein the plurality of sequencing regions comprises at least four sequencing regions, and the plurality of re-phrasing regions comprises at last three re-phrasing regions. 58 73. The method of any one of claims 66-72, wherein the primers are extended through the sequencing region for a predetermined number of sequencing flow steps. 74. The method of claim 73, wherein the predetermined number of sequencing flow steps is between about 40 and about 200. 75. The method of claim 73 or 74, wherein the predetermined number of sequencing flow steps is associated with a predetermined sequencing signal threshold. 76. The method of claim 73 or 74, wherein the predetermined number of sequencing flow steps is associated with an expected proportion of unsynchronized primers. 77. The method of claim 76, wherein the expected proportion of unsynchronized primers is between about 0.5 and about 0.9. 78. The method of claim 73 or 74, wherein the predetermined number of sequencing flow steps is associated with an expected length of the sequencing region. 79. The method of any one of claims 66-72, wherein the primers are extended through the sequencing region until a sequencing signal falls below a predetermined sequencing signal threshold. 80. The method of any one of claims 66-79, wherein a mixture of three different types of nucleotide bases are used in at least one of the one or more re-phasing flow steps. 81. The method of any one of claims 66-80, wherein at least a portion of nucleotides in the one or more re-phasing flow steps are unlabeled. 82. The method of any one of claims 66-81, wherein nucleotides in the one or more rephasing flow steps are unlabeled. 83. The method of any one of claims 66-82, wherein the distance between the start of the first sequencing region and the end of the final sequencing region is between about 300 bases in length and about 5000 bases in length. 59 84. The method of any one of claims 33-83, wherein nucleotides used to extend the primers are non-terminating nucleotides. 85. The method of any one of claims 33-84, wherein at least a portion of the nucleotides used in the re-phasing flow steps are unlabeled. 86. The method of any one of claims 33-85, wherein nucleotides used in the re-phasing flow steps are unlabeled. 87. The method of any one of claims 33-86, wherein nucleotides used in the one or more re-phasing flow steps are used at a higher concentration than nucleotides used in the sequencing flow steps. 88. The method of any one of claims 33-87, wherein the polynucleotide copies are attached to a rotatable substrate. 60
